The base angles of an isosceles triangle are equal.
The bisector of the apex angle of an isosceles triangle bisects the base perpendicularly.
A triangle with equal base angles is an isosceles triangle.

If the diagonals of a quadrilateral intersect at the midpoint, it is a parallelogram.
The opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal. The opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal in length.

If a pair of opposite sides of a quadrilateral are parallel and of equal length, it is a parallelogram.
